title: US Crude Oil Inventories Keep Falling As Hormuz Flows Slow To Start
summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 6.072 million barrels in the week ending June 26. title: China's Energy Future Runs on Coal and Solar
summary: China will continue spending heavily on both coal and alternative energy, such as wind and solar, over the next five years, Beijing’s latest five-year plan revealed this month. title: The Oil Benchmark Named Brent Is Losing Its Brent
summary: The world's most important oil benchmark is about to experience an identity crisis. title: US Crude Output Sets New Monthly Production Record
summary: According to monthly data released Tuesday by the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA), U.S. crude oil production climbed to a record 13.934 million barrels per day (bpd) in April—the highest monthly production rate ever recorded.
